description_long: This is a delicate, open flower with soft, ruffled petals that display a gradient of lavender to white hues. The petals are thin and slightly translucent, with gentle undulations and natural imperfections along the edges. At the center, a cluster of reproductive structures is visible, surrounded by the innermost petals. The flower is attached to a short, greenish stem that is partially visible beneath the bloom. The overall form is cup-like, with the petals curving outward and upward, creating a sense of depth and volume. The coloration and petal arrangement suggest a species similar to a Lisianthus or a delicate garden bloom often found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A soft, lavender and white flower with ruffled, semi-translucent petals and a short green stem. The bloom is open and cup-shaped, revealing a detailed central structure.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open face of the flower, with the central reproductive structures and the layered, ruffled petals most visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the depth of the bloom and the upward curve of the petals, with the stem more visible beneath the flower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the outer surface of the petals and the base of the flower, with the stem clearly visible and no central structures exposed.
description_view_3: Side-rear view emphasizing the curvature and layering of the petals, with the stem and the back of the flower prominent.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled configuration, overlapping and radiating outward from the center in multiple layers, with some irregularity and ruffling along the edges.
